title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Automation and Data Client Systems · Python Build Room
summary: A civic-data automation team integrating a staging catalog must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers typed response and failure classifier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Defend the transferred model · Workshop Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — civic-data staging importer observes 202 without a result, 204 with no content, 206 with a range, 429 with Retry-After, and 503 problem+json for case P-4. Preserve status, transport, schema, domain, partial, and unknown-effect outcomes separately. Competency probe: Represent partial results and unknown write outcomes so callers do not silently retry or declare success. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Evidence target: Python Defend the transferred model · Workshop Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes.
steps.why: Retrieving Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes through the concrete a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers typed response and failure classifier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Python Defend the transferred model · Workshop Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes, keep Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Represent partial results and unknown write outcomes so callers do not silently retry or declare success. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
